text:
Rorgo (ローゴ, Roogo) is an antagonist in the video game Hunter × Hunter: Each One's Determination with connections to the Four Heavenly Kings and the darker side of Sunaka. He appears in Kurapika's route and briefly Leorio's. He is interested in the Scarlet Eyes and is one of the mafiosos in Sunaka. Hunter × Hunter: Each One's Determination 

Appearance

Rorgo is an extremely tall and muscular middle-aged man with tanned skin, buzzcut-styled blond hair in a short ponytail held by a dark brown holder with a red streak in the front, narrow black eyes, bushy eyebrows, and a sharp mustache. His outfit consists of light blue martial arts attire with teal stitching on the chest, paired with a red sash and black pants and shoes.

Personality

Rorgo always acts like a man of few words. When in public, he puts on a formal facade. However, he is a cruel and sadistic criminal who just wants to harm others to archive its goals. He also has a violent obsession for fighting, as shown when he desires to fight Kurapika alone, refusing to kill him when Tron and Izunavi get involved. 

Background

One of Don Gappai's Four Heavenly Kings, Rorgo is obsessed with collecting eyeballs akin to Neon. He also leads his own group of men, consisting of Doro, Shives, Surirao, and Donal. Rorgo and his subordinates are all Nen users. Prior to the game event, Rorgo met Chrollo. This would cause him to become interested in the troupe and the Scarlet Eyes. 

Cyber Mobile

LV 10
HP 200
マフィアのボスにつかえる四天王のうちの一人。
放出系のうりょくをもっている。
念でつくりだした七のボーメランを使う。
 

|-|Translated English Text=
 
LV 10
HP 200
One of the Four Heavenly Kings, a part of the mafia.
He is skilled in Emission.
He uses seven aura-based boomerangs as weapons.
 
 

Plot

Each One's Determination

 Note: Events occurring in Each One's Determination are not canon material. 

Rorgo is first mentioned in Kurapika's first chapter. The rookie hunter at a local Cyber Cafe seeks more information on Sunaka. After unlocking the website, various results appear. Among them are those of a man named Rorgo and pages on the Scarlet Eyes. Kurapika concludes that Rorgo has some form of connection to them. Later, as he talks to Clario, she accidentally reveals that he is a guest attending the VIP party. A morning news report further confirms this. When Kurapika tracks down Sansho, the fake hunter threatens to involve Rorgo, which only angers the former. Here, Sansho claims that he does, in fact, have the eyes and runs off. 

At the VIP party, a disguised Kurapika briefly talks to Rorgo. He attempts to question him about his collection of eyeballs. However, this only results in Rorgo dismissing it as a 'trivial hobby.' After Clario is found and chased, she accidentally tears apart the collector's shirt. On his back, a spider tattoo is visible. Unbeknownst to Kurapika, this causes his eyes to turn scarlet. 

Sometime after Kurapika finishes his Nen training, Rorgo kidnaps Clario. This is from him mistaking her for Kurapika. Learning the truth, he considers killing her until he realizes she could be used as bait. Thus, he keeps her alive and has her interrogated. After Kurapika and Clario run away from the collapsing mansion, Rorgo follows them. Kurapika attempts to use his newfound chain abilities against him. However, they are easily destroyed because they lack any Nen restrictions. Tron and Izunavi are forced to step in to help. Rorgo declares that he will be at Logos Island, waiting for Kurapika. The group thus manages to escape from Rorgo. 

A week later, Kurapika arrives via boat. He is told by Majtani that Rorgo refuses to fight him until he deals with his henchmen first. After defeating each one, Kurapika finds a spider tattoo with a code letter and a message. The boy presumes Rorgo is trying to anger him and make his eyes turn scarlet. Once he uses the code at a nearby waterfall, Rorgo appears at the top. The duo proceeds to fight. After the first round, Rorgo commends him, claiming that the Phantom Troupe struggled to kill the other Kurta Clan members. This leads Kurapika to realize he knows the brigade. Before he can question him, the mafioso takes the opportunity to use the Seventh Boomerang multiple times. 

After a few more rounds, Kurapika questions him one last time about his connection to the troupe and his spider tattoo. This gives Rorgo the idea to lie about being a member, outright claiming to have helped during the Kurta Clan massacre. This results in Kurapika becoming enraged and using Emperor Time against him. With Rorgo properly defeated, Kurapika demands to know where the other members are. This causes Rorgo to admit he is not actually a member. After meeting them, he became inspired and wished to join their ranks. He describes himself as a fan of theirs. When Kurapika begins questioning him on his Scarlet Eyes collection, Rorgo jumps into the nearby waterfall. Believing him to be dead, Kurapika somberly leaves. 

While Kurapika attempts to be hired by the Nostrade family, it is revealed that Rorgo survived. Once Kurapika obtains Sara's hair, the two run into each other near the family's mansion. Rorgo claims to be there for Don Gappai, his boss. He then comments that the Kurta member underestimated him and proceeds to fight him for multiple rounds. Rorgo then attempts to use his black boomerang against him. However, the younger boy catches it and survives. As Rorgo approaches, Kurapika then uses Emperor Time. After defeating him, Rorgo is questioned about the Scarlet Eyes. Rorgo claims to have never had them to begin with, then taunts Kurapika. He then passes out in front of him. 

In Leorio's route, Rorgo is briefly seen talking to Donal. The duo discusses the cloning procedures conducted by Donal, with Rorgo noting that he did not meet his quota. He orders him to get to work and leaves the place after his defeat. He is never seen again after that. 

Abilities & Powers

In-game, he is skilled in the likes of Nen and can overpower his opponents through his physical strength. His martial arts outfit implies that he is proficient in the field. He is also occasionally shown using a knife in battle. In his Cyber Mobile description, he is listed as an Emitter.

Nen

Trivia

In the relationship chart for the game, Rorgo and Chrollo's relationship is described as "(Chrollo) sold the Scarlet Eyes to him" (緋の眼を売る, Hi no me wo uru). However, Rorgo claims to have never had them to begin with. From the fact that he lied previously, his knowing Chrollo, and Sansho stating he owns a collection of them, it can be speculated that he lied again so Kurapika would spare him. autisticlio "Translated Each One's Determination relationship chart" Tumblr 
When first met at the VIP party, Rorgo uses the personal pronoun 'watakushi', which is associated with a higher-class person. However, later encounters have him use 'ore,' which is typically used by masculine characters.
Despite his artwork depicting him with yellow boomerangs, Rorgo is stated not to have that particular color.

Intertextuality and References

The Four Heavenly Kings (四天王, Shitennō) in Buddhist beliefs are four protective deities who separately watch over a specific cardinal direction of the world. That specific deity guards each direction. They have been referenced in various Japanese media, such as the Megami Tensei games and the manga Gintama.
